title: Renting an Apartment
summary: A tenant visits an apartment with a landlord, asks questions about the property and lease terms, and negotiates a few details before deciding whether to take the flat.

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: Come on in. This is the living room — as you can see, it gets a lot of natural light in the mornings.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: It's lovely. The space is bigger than I expected from the photos. How old is the building?

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: It was built in the 1970s, but the flat was completely renovated about five years ago. All the appliances are relatively new.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: That's good to know. Are bills included in the rent, or are they separate?

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: The rent is eight hundred a month, and that includes water and building maintenance. Electricity and gas are separate — you'd set up your own accounts for those.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: I see. And what about internet? Is there already a connection here?

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: There's a broadband line installed, but you'd need to arrange your own contract with a provider. It usually takes about a week to set up.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: That's fine. Can I ask about parking? I have a car.

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: There's a resident's parking area behind the building. One space is included with the flat. If you ever need a second space, they're available to rent separately.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: Perfect. One more question — is the flat pet-friendly? I have a small cat.

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: We do allow cats, yes. I just ask that any damage to the property is covered by the tenant. The deposit is one month's rent, which is refundable if the flat is left in good condition.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: That seems fair. What is the minimum lease term?

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: We normally ask for a twelve-month contract. After that, it can roll over to a monthly arrangement if both parties are happy.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: Would it be possible to repaint one of the bedrooms? The colour is a bit dark for my taste.

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: I'd need you to use a neutral colour and restore it to the original shade before you leave. But yes, that should be fine as long as you let me know in advance.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: Of course. I think this flat could work very well for me. Can I have until tomorrow to think it over and confirm?

body.speaker: Landlord
body.text: Absolutely. Take your time. Just give me a call or send a message by tomorrow evening and we can get the paperwork started.

body.speaker: Tenant
body.text: Thank you very much. I'll be in touch.

annotations.exampleEn: The studio artist prefers rooms with plenty of natural light.

annotations.meaningVi: được cải tạo hoàn toàn, sửa chữa toàn bộ
annotations.exampleEn: The old farmhouse was completely renovated before the family moved in.

annotations.meaningVi: đã bao gồm hóa đơn tiện ích, tiện ích đã tính vào tiền thuê
annotations.exampleEn: Some student flats advertise bills included to make budgeting easier.

annotations.meaningVi: tự mở tài khoản, tự ký hợp đồng dịch vụ
annotations.exampleEn: When you move into a new flat, you'll need to set up your own accounts for utilities.

annotations.meaningVi: đường truyền internet băng thông rộng
annotations.exampleEn: The landlord confirmed that a broadband line had already been installed.

annotations.meaningVi: khu vực đỗ xe dành cho cư dân
annotations.exampleEn: The resident's parking area behind the building is available to tenants only.

annotations.meaningVi: thân thiện với thú cưng, cho phép nuôi thú cưng
annotations.exampleEn: We specifically looked for a pet-friendly flat when we adopted our dog.

annotations.meaningVi: tiền đặt cọc có thể hoàn trả
annotations.exampleEn: The landlord explained that the refundable deposit would be returned within two weeks of moving out.

annotations.meaningVi: thời hạn hợp đồng tối thiểu
annotations.exampleEn: The minimum lease term is six months, with the option to renew after that.

annotations.phrase: roll over to a monthly arrangement
annotations.meaningVi: chuyển sang hợp đồng hàng tháng, tự động gia hạn theo tháng
annotations.exampleEn: If neither party gives notice, the contract will roll over to a monthly arrangement.

annotations.meaningVi: màu trung tính, màu nhạt không nổi bật
annotations.exampleEn: Landlords usually prefer neutral colours on walls as they appeal to more tenants.

annotations.phrase: restore it to the original shade
annotations.meaningVi: khôi phục về màu sắc ban đầu
annotations.exampleEn: When moving out, tenants are required to restore the walls to the original shade.
Please let us know in advance if you plan to make any changes to the flat.

annotations.meaningVi: suy nghĩ lại, cân nhắc kỹ
annotations.exampleEn: She asked for a day to think it over before signing the contract.

annotations.meaningVi: bắt đầu làm thủ tục giấy tờ
annotations.exampleEn: Once you confirm, we can get the paperwork started right away.

grammarNotes.title: Conditional Type 1 — Discussing terms and conditions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 được dùng nhiều trong các cuộc đàm phán về hợp đồng và điều khoản thuê nhà để diễn đạt những gì có thể hoặc sẽ xảy ra trong một tình huống nhất định. Ví dụ: "if the flat is left in good condition" (tiền cọc sẽ được hoàn trả), "as long as you let me know in advance" (bạn có thể sơn lại phòng). Cấu trúc: if/as long as + hiện tại đơn, will/can + động từ. 'As long as' và 'provided that' là những cách thay thế cho 'if' khi đặt điều kiện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Type 1 conditionals appear naturally in rental negotiations when discussing conditions and consequences. 'As long as you let me know in advance' (if + present simple → permission granted) and 'if the flat is left in good condition' (implied: the deposit will be returned) are examples. 'As long as' and 'provided that' are B1+ alternatives to 'if' that introduce conditions.

grammarNotes.title: Modal Verbs — Expressing requests and permissions politely
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong hội thoại thuê nhà, các động từ khuyết thiếu được dùng để hỏi xin phép và đề nghị một cách lịch sự. Ví dụ: "Can I ask about parking?" (hỏi lịch sự), "Would it be possible to repaint one of the bedrooms?" (yêu cầu lịch sự hơn), "I'd need you to use a neutral colour" ('would' + need = yêu cầu có chừng mực). 'Would it be possible to' là cách diễn đạt yêu cầu lịch sự hơn 'Can I', phù hợp khi nói chuyện với người mới gặp.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Modal verbs create a gradient of politeness in requests. 'Can I ask?' is direct but acceptable. 'Would it be possible to repaint?' is softer and more respectful — appropriate when asking a landlord for something that might be refused. 'I'd need you to' (= 'I would need you to') is a polite way to state a requirement without sounding demanding. Recognising these gradations is key for B1 learners in formal contexts.

translationVi:
Chủ nhà: Mời vào. Đây là phòng khách — như bạn thấy, buổi sáng có rất nhiều ánh sáng tự nhiên.
Người thuê: Đẹp lắm. Không gian rộng hơn tôi nghĩ qua ảnh. Tòa nhà xây năm nào vậy?
Chủ nhà: Được xây vào những năm 1970, nhưng căn hộ đã được cải tạo hoàn toàn khoảng năm năm trước. Tất cả thiết bị đều tương đối mới.
Người thuê: Tốt quá. Hóa đơn có bao gồm trong tiền thuê không, hay tính riêng?
Chủ nhà: Tiền thuê là tám trăm một tháng, đã bao gồm nước và bảo trì tòa nhà. Điện và gas tính riêng — bạn phải tự mở tài khoản dịch vụ đó.
Người thuê: Tôi hiểu rồi. Còn internet thì sao? Ở đây đã có kết nối chưa?
Chủ nhà: Đã có đường truyền băng thông rộng được lắp sẵn, nhưng bạn cần tự ký hợp đồng với nhà cung cấp. Thường mất khoảng một tuần để thiết lập.
Người thuê: Được thôi. Tôi muốn hỏi về chỗ đỗ xe. Tôi có xe.
Chủ nhà: Có khu vực đỗ xe dành cho cư dân phía sau tòa nhà. Một chỗ đã được tính vào căn hộ. Nếu bạn cần chỗ thứ hai, có thể thuê thêm riêng.
Người thuê: Tuyệt vời. Tôi còn một câu hỏi nữa — căn hộ có cho phép nuôi thú cưng không? Tôi có một con mèo nhỏ.
Chủ nhà: Chúng tôi cho phép nuôi mèo, có. Tôi chỉ yêu cầu bất kỳ thiệt hại nào đối với tài sản phải được người thuê chi trả. Tiền đặt cọc là một tháng tiền thuê, có thể hoàn trả nếu căn hộ được trả lại trong tình trạng tốt.
Người thuê: Có vẻ hợp lý. Thời hạn hợp đồng tối thiểu là bao lâu?
Chủ nhà: Chúng tôi thường yêu cầu hợp đồng mười hai tháng. Sau đó, có thể chuyển sang hợp đồng hàng tháng nếu cả hai bên đều hài lòng.
Người thuê: Có thể sơn lại một trong các phòng ngủ không? Màu sắc hơi tối so với gu của tôi.
Chủ nhà: Tôi cần bạn dùng màu trung tính và khôi phục lại màu gốc trước khi chuyển đi. Nhưng vâng, được thôi miễn là bạn báo tôi trước.
Người thuê: Tất nhiên rồi. Tôi nghĩ căn hộ này rất phù hợp với tôi. Tôi có thể có đến ngày mai để suy nghĩ và xác nhận không?
Chủ nhà: Hoàn toàn được. Cứ từ từ. Chỉ cần gọi hoặc nhắn cho tôi vào buổi tối mai là chúng ta có thể bắt đầu làm thủ tục giấy tờ.
Người thuê: Cảm ơn rất nhiều. Tôi sẽ liên lạc.

questions.prompt: What does the landlord say about the morning light in the flat?
options: The flat has too many windows and can get very bright
The flat gets a lot of natural light in the mornings
The flat is mainly sunny in the afternoons
The flat needs better curtains to manage the light
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'it gets a lot of natural light in the mornings.'
questions.hint: The landlord opens by describing a positive feature of the living room.

questions.prompt: When was the flat last renovated?
options: When the building was first constructed in the 1970s
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'the flat was completely renovated about five years ago.'
questions.hint: The landlord distinguishes between the age of the building and the renovation date.

questions.prompt: What does the monthly rent of eight hundred include?
options: All utilities including electricity and broadband
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'that includes water and building maintenance' — electricity and gas are separate.
questions.hint: Listen carefully to which utilities are included and which are not.

questions.prompt: What does the tenant need to do about internet access?
options: Nothing — internet is fully set up and included in the rent
Ask the landlord to install a new broadband line
Arrange their own contract with a provider using the existing broadband line
Pay an extra monthly fee to the landlord for internet access
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'there's a broadband line installed, but you'd need to arrange your own contract with a provider.'
questions.hint: The infrastructure exists but the contract does not.

questions.prompt: What parking arrangement is included with the flat?
options: No parking is available — the tenant must park on the street
Two parking spaces are included with the flat
One parking space is included, with additional spaces available to rent
A garage is included for an extra monthly fee
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'One space is included with the flat. If you ever need a second space, they're available to rent separately.'
questions.hint: One space is free; extras cost extra.

questions.prompt: Under what condition does the landlord allow a cat in the flat?
options: No conditions — all pets are welcome
Only if the tenant pays an extra monthly fee
Only if the cat is kept in one room
Any damage caused must be covered by the tenant
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'I just ask that any damage to the property is covered by the tenant.'
questions.hint: The condition relates to responsibility for damage.

questions.prompt: Under what circumstances will the deposit be refunded?
options: After one year regardless of the flat's condition
Only if the tenant stays for more than two years
If the flat is left in good condition
If the tenant gives three months' notice before leaving
questions.explanation: The landlord says the deposit 'is refundable if the flat is left in good condition.'
questions.hint: The condition for refund is about the state of the property.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum lease term the landlord requires?
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'We normally ask for a twelve-month contract.'
questions.hint: The landlord states a specific duration.

questions.prompt: What happens to the contract after the initial lease term?
options: It automatically ends and the tenant must move out
It can roll over to a monthly arrangement if both parties agree
The landlord can increase the rent by any amount
The tenant must sign another twelve-month contract
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'After that, it can roll over to a monthly arrangement if both parties are happy.'
questions.hint: Think about the flexible option after the initial twelve months.

questions.prompt: What conditions does the landlord set for repainting a bedroom?
options: The tenant must hire a professional painter approved by the landlord
The tenant must use a neutral colour and restore it before leaving
The tenant cannot paint the flat under any circumstances
The tenant must pay the landlord to have it repainted professionally
questions.explanation: The landlord says 'I'd need you to use a neutral colour and restore it to the original shade before you leave.'
questions.hint: Two conditions are set: the type of colour and the requirement to restore it.

questions.prompt: What does the tenant ask for before making a final decision?
options: A discount on the first month's rent
A copy of the lease agreement to review
Until tomorrow to think it over and confirm
A second viewing of the flat at a different time of day
questions.explanation: The tenant says 'Can I have until tomorrow to think it over and confirm?'
questions.hint: The tenant wants a short period to consider before committing.

cloze.template: Landlord: Come on in. This is the living room — it gets a lot of {{b1}} light in the mornings. Tenant: It's lovely. How old is the building? Landlord: It was built in the 1970s, but the flat was completely {{b2}} about five years ago. Tenant: Are bills included in the rent, or are they separate? Landlord: The rent is eight hundred a month, and that includes water and building {{b3}}. Electricity and gas are separate — you'd {{b4}} your own accounts for those. Tenant: Is there already an internet connection here? Landlord: There's a {{b5}} line installed, but you'd need to arrange your own contract with a provider. Tenant: Can I ask about parking? Landlord: There's a resident's parking area behind the building. One space is {{b6}} with the flat. Tenant: Is the flat {{b7}}? I have a small cat. Landlord: We do allow cats. Any damage to the property must be covered by the tenant. The {{b8}} is one month's rent, which is refundable if the flat is left in good condition. Tenant: What is the minimum {{b9}} term? Landlord: We normally ask for a twelve-month contract. After that, it can {{b10}} to a monthly arrangement. Tenant: Would it be possible to repaint one of the bedrooms? Landlord: I'd need you to use a {{b11}} colour and restore it to the original shade before you leave.

blanks.explanation: 'Natural light' is the standard phrase for sunlight entering through windows — a key selling point for residential properties.
'Completely renovated' means thoroughly rebuilt and updated — the most natural phrasing for a full refurbishment.
'Building maintenance' refers to the ongoing upkeep and repair of communal areas and building systems — a standard cost included in some rents.
'Set up your own accounts' is the natural phrasal verb for registering with a utility provider — 'set up' is standard in administrative contexts.
'Broadband line' is the specific term for a high-speed internet connection infrastructure — the exact word used by the landlord.
'Included with the flat' means it comes as part of the rental without extra cost — the most natural phrasing in property descriptions.
'Pet-friendly' is the standard hyphenated adjective used in rental listings to indicate that animals are allowed.
'Deposit' is the standard term for a sum paid before moving in that is returned at the end of the tenancy if there is no damage.
'Lease term' is the standard legal phrase for the duration of a rental agreement — 'minimum lease term' specifies the shortest period available.
'Roll over to' is the specific phrasal verb used for a contract that automatically continues on different terms after its initial period ends.
'Neutral colour' specifically refers to a non-bold, broadly acceptable shade — a standard requirement in rental agreements when tenants are allowed to redecorate.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The landlord and tenant in this conversation manage to reach agreement on several points through polite negotiation. In your experience, what are the most important things to check or negotiate when renting a property? Do you think renters have enough legal protection in most countries, or do landlords generally have too much power?
